metadata.title: Guideline on Reporting the Results of Population Pharmacokinetic Analyses

rules.rule_text: A population PK report must include sufficient detail to enable a secondary evaluation by regulatory authorities of the conducted analysis and conclusions drawn.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline provides guidance on how to present the results of a population pharmacokinetic analysis, in order to provide a level of details that will enable a secondary evaluation (i.e. assessment by regulatory authorities of the conducted analysis and conclusions drawn).

rules.rule_text: Every assumption and decision made during model development must be made clear for the regulatory assessor.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is therefore vital that every assumption and decision made during model development is made clear for the assessor.

rules.rule_text: This guideline applies to all Marketing Authorisation Applications for human medicinal products submitted in accordance with Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline applies to all Marketing Authorisation Applications for human medicinal products submitted in accordance with Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.
cross_references: Annex I to Directive 2001/83/83 Part I Module 5

rules.rule_text: This guideline must be read in conjunction with the Introduction and general principles paragraph (4) and Part I, Module 5 of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/83 as amended, and all other pertinent elements outlined in current and future EU and ICH guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline should be read in conjunction with the Introduction and general principles paragraph (4) and Part I, Module 5 of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83 as amended, and all other pertinent elements outlined in current and future EU and ICH guidelines and regulations especially those on: Pharmacokinetic Studies in Man (3CC3A, 1987); The Investigation of Drug Interactions (CPMP/EWP/560/95); Evaluation of the Pharmacokinetics of Medicinal Products in Patients with Impaired Renal Function (CPMP/EWP/225/02); Evaluation of the Pharmacokinetics of Medicinal Products in Patients with Impaired Hepatic Function (CPMP/EWP/2339/02); Guideline on the Role of Pharmacokinetics in the development of Medicinal Products in the Paediatric Population (CPMP/EWP/147013/2004); Guideline on Clinical Investigation if the Pharmacokinetics of Therapeutic Proteins (CPMP/EWP/89249/04).

rules.rule_text: There must be a prospectively written analysis plan for the population PK analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: There should be a prospectively written analysis plan for the population PK analysis.
rules.exceptions: It is acknowledged that the level of information in the analysis plan may be less detailed than in a standard clinical protocol, due in part to the exploratory nature of some population analyses.

rules.rule_text: The analysis plan must be presented and could form an appendix in the report of the population PK analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The analysis plan should be presented and could form an appendix in the report of the population PK analysis.

rules.rule_text: The analysis plan must at least include: the objective(s) of the analysis; a brief description of the study or studies from which data originate; the nature of the data to be analyzed (number of subjects, rich or sparsely sampled); procedures for handling missing data and outlying data; general modelling aspects (software, estimation methods, diagnostics); overall modelling procedure/strategy; structural models to be tested; variability models to be tested; covariates and covariate models to be tested with rationale; algorithms/methods for covariate model building; criteria for model selection and covariate inclusion; and model evaluation/qualification procedures.
rules.verbatim_quote: the analysis plan should at least include: the objective(s) of the analysis; a brief description of the study (or studies) from which the data originate; the nature of the data to be analyzed (how many subjects, rich or sparsely sampled); the procedures for handling missing data and outlying data; the general modelling aspects (e.g. software, estimation methods, diagnostics); the overall modelling procedure/strategy; the structural models to be tested (if this has been decided); the variability models to be tested; the covariates and covariate models to be tested together with a rationale for testing these covariates based on, for example, biological, pharmacological and/or clinical plausibility.; the algorithms/methods to be used for covariate model building; the criteria to be used for selection of models during model building and inclusion of covariates (e.g. objective function value, level of statistical significance, goodness of fit plots, standard error, inter-individual variability, clinical relevance); the model evaluation/qualification procedures to be used.
rules.exceptions: Level of detail may be less than a standard clinical protocol given exploratory nature of some analyses.

rules.rule_text: References to specific methodologies used must be given, and when relevant included in the submitted documentation.
rules.verbatim_quote: References to specific methodologies used should be given, and when relevant included in the documentation submitted.

rules.rule_text: Documents already included in other parts of the submitted documentation need not be appended; clear cross-references must be given, preferably with hyperlinks in an eCTD application.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is not necessary to append documents that are already included in other parts of the submitted documentation (as study protocols, analytical reports etc.). Clear cross-references should be given to these documents and preferably with hyperlinks in an eCTD application.

rules.rule_text: The summary section must provide an overall summary including context of the study, objectives, study design, data (number of subjects and samples), methods, results and main findings and conclusions.
rules.verbatim_quote: The summary should provide an overall summary of the population PK analysis. It should include sufficient information on the context of the study, objectives, study design, data (number of subjects and samples), methods, results and the main findings and conclusions of the population PK analysis.

rules.rule_text: The introduction section must provide background information about the drug, the intent of the analysis, and place the population PK analysis in its proper context within the drug's clinical development, indicating any special features of the population PK analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The introduction in a population PK report should provide some background information about the drug to be analyzed and the intent of the analysis. It should include sufficient background information to place the population PK analysis in its proper context within the drug's clinical development and indicate any special features of the population PK analysis.

rules.rule_text: The objectives of the analysis must be stated in the report.
rules.verbatim_quote: The objectives of the analysis should be stated.

rules.rule_text: The data section must briefly describe the study or studies from which the data included in the analysis originate, including nominal number of samples per subject per visit and sampling times in each study.
rules.verbatim_quote: The report should briefly describe the study or studies from which the data included in the analysis originate. Information regarding nominal number of samples per subject per visit and sampling times in each study should be given.

rules.rule_text: The method for calculation of derived covariates (e.g. creatinine clearance, ideal body weight, body mass index) must be stated.
rules.verbatim_quote: The method for calculation of derived covariates (e.g. creatinine clearance, ideal body weight, body mass index) should be stated.

rules.rule_text: When relevant, the type of data transformation used must be described and justified, applying to both the dependent variable and other variables.
rules.verbatim_quote: When relevant, the type of data transformation used should be described and justified. This applies both to the dependent variable and other variables.

rules.rule_text: Handling of missing data must be described, including procedures for missing covariate information, missing dose and/or sample times. The handling of data below the limit of quantification must also be described and possible consequences discussed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Handling of missing data should be described. For example not all subjects may have complete covariate information and other subjects may have missing dose and/or sample times. The procedures taken in the case of missing data should be fully described in the report. The handling of data below the limit of quantification should also be described and possible consequences discussed (censoring problems).

rules.rule_text: The report must describe and justify procedures taken in handling outliers, including rules applied for omitting data completely or for re-inclusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: Outliers are usually identified on initial visual inspection of raw data, and/or inspection of the output from initial model building runs. The report should describe and justify the procedures that were taken in handling outliers, e.g. description of rules applied for omitting data completely or for re-inclusion.

rules.rule_text: Electronic files of the analysis datasets must be provided as comma separated and space delimited text files.
rules.verbatim_quote: Electronic files of the analysis datasets should be provided as comma separated and space delimited text files.

rules.rule_text: A specification of the datasets used must be included, clearly describing differences between various datasets used, and referenced in the run record.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to include a specification of the data sets used, clearly describing the differences between various data sets used, and to be referenced in the run record.

rules.rule_text: The methods section must describe the same components as the analysis plan, and any deviations from the analysis plan must be clearly emphasised.
rules.verbatim_quote: The methods section should describe the methods used and should include the same components as the analysis plan. If, during the analysis, any deviations from the analysis plan occur, these should be clearly emphasised in the methods section of the report.

rules.rule_text: The methods section must include information regarding the type of bioanalytical methods used and the limit of quantification for each analyte in each method.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section should also include information regarding the type of bioanalytical methods used and the limit of quantification for each analyte in each method.

rules.rule_text: The choice of analysis (e.g. parametric maximum likelihood, non-parametric, Bayesian) and the choice of estimation method (e.g. FO, FOCE, FOCE INTER) must be stated and justified. The software and version used must be stated. Assumptions made during the analysis must be stated and briefly discussed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The choice of analysis (e.g. parametric maximum likelihood, non-parametric maximum likelihood, Bayesian) and the choice of estimation method (e.g. FO, FOCE, FOCE INTER) should be stated and justified. The software and version used should be stated. Assumptions made during the analysis should be stated and briefly discussed [6].

rules.rule_text: The criteria used for selection of models during model building and inclusion of covariates (e.g. objective function value, level of statistical significance, goodness of fit plots, standard error, inter-individual variability, clinical relevance) must be described, and it is recommended to justify the statistical criteria used.
rules.verbatim_quote: The criteria to be used for selection of models during model building and inclusion of covariates (e.g. objective function value, level of statistical significance, goodness of fit plots, standard error, inter-individual variability, clinical relevance) should be described. It is recommended to justify the statistical criteria used, e.g. LRT, AIC or other.
rules.exceptions: When using the LRT the actual statistical significance level (ΔOFV in NONMEM) could be markedly different from the nominal, depending on the estimation method used and number of subjects, number of samples per subject, residual error magnitude.

rules.rule_text: The report must clearly present a priori information available regarding the potential structure of the model, and a description of models evaluated must be given.
rules.verbatim_quote: The report should clearly present a priori information available regarding the potential structure of the model. A description of models evaluated should be given.

rules.rule_text: Covariates to be tested for inclusion in the model must be presented together with a rationale based on biological, pharmacological and/or clinical plausibility. The parameterisation of the covariate model must be specified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The covariates to be tested for inclusion in the model should be presented together with a rationale for testing these covariates based on, for example, biological, pharmacological and/or clinical plausibility. The parameterisation of the covariate model should be specified.

rules.rule_text: The covariate model building procedure must be described, and criteria for covariate selection (e.g. statistical significance and clinical relevance) must be presented.
rules.verbatim_quote: The covariate model building procedure (e.g. step-wise covariate model building procedures [8]) should be described. The criteria for covariate selection (e.g. statistical significance and clinical relevance) should be presented.

rules.rule_text: Models applied to describe inter-individual, residual and, when relevant, inter-occasion variability must be described.
rules.verbatim_quote: Models applied to describe inter-individual, residual and, when relevant, inter-occasion variability should be described.
rules.exceptions: Inter-occasion variability description only required when relevant.

rules.rule_text: Model evaluation procedures must be described and may include both graphical evaluation and statistical procedures. The report must contain justification for the model evaluation procedures and tools used for the specific evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The model evaluation procedures should be described and may include both graphical evaluation and statistical procedures. The report should contain justification for the model evaluation procedures and tools used for the specific evaluation.
rules.exceptions: Model evaluation procedures will depend upon the objective(s) of the model development; procedures supporting description of data and covariate effects could be simpler than those needed if the final model is to be used to perform simulations for dosage recommendations.

rules.rule_text: In the case of substantial simulations based on the model, these must be described in detail, including description of the demographics (e.g. covariate distribution and variability) of the simulation data set.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of substantial simulations based on the model, these should be described in detail, including description of the demographics (e.g. covariate distribution and variability) of the simulation data set.
rules.exceptions: Only required when substantial simulations are performed.

rules.rule_text: The results section must include the following description of the data: total number of subjects; total number of observed concentrations used; distribution of samples; plots of raw data on linear scale and usually also on log-linear scale; separate presentation of data from internal or external validation datasets.
rules.verbatim_quote: The report should include the following description of the data: The total number of subjects; The total number of observed concentrations used in the analysis.; The distribution of samples presented in an appropriate way that might include tables or graphics (e.g. histograms) presenting the actual number of samples per subject sorted by study, treatment group, visit etc.; Plots of the raw data provided on linear scale and usually also on log-linear scale. Lines connecting the data points could be included for part of the subjects to visualise the data.; Separate presentation of data from internal or external validation datasets.

rules.rule_text: The results data section must include information regarding drop-outs during the study (number, time point in relation to PK sampling, and when relevant, reason for drop out).
rules.verbatim_quote: Information regarding drop-outs during the study (number of drop-outs, time point in relation to PK sampling, and when relevant, reason for drop out).
rules.exceptions: Reason for drop-out only required when relevant.

rules.rule_text: The results data section must include summary statistics and histograms of continuous covariates and frequencies of categorical covariates, stratified over subpopulations or validation sets if relevant.
rules.verbatim_quote: Summary statistics and histograms of the continuous covariates and frequencies of categorical covariates. If relevant, this covariate data could be presented stratified over subpopulations or validation sets.
rules.exceptions: Stratification optional and only if relevant.

rules.rule_text: The results data section must include correlation between covariates, missing data (missing dosing and sample times, missing covariates), outliers specified with all relevant data available, and subjects removed from the analysis listed with relevant patient characteristics.
rules.verbatim_quote: Correlation between covariates. Missing data (missing dosing and sample times, missing covariates). Outliers specified with all relevant data available. Subjects removed from the analysis listed with relevant patient characteristics.

rules.rule_text: The report must describe any major decisions taken during base model development and include an overview of steps taken (a run record) that clearly describes important decisions during building of the base population model. The use of abbreviations or codes in the run record that are difficult to interpret must be avoided.
rules.verbatim_quote: The report should describe any major decisions taken during the base model development and should include an overview of the steps taken during model development (a run record) that, at a minimum, clearly describes important decisions taken during the building of the base population model. The run record may be presented in a separate appendix. The use of abbreviations or codes in the run record that are difficult to interpret should be avoided.
rules.exceptions: The run record may be presented in a separate appendix.

rules.rule_text: The run record must describe changes from the previous model and decisions taken, and could include a brief but interpretable description of the run, objective function value, and information whether the model converged successfully. Preferably it should also include parameter estimates for key runs.
rules.verbatim_quote: The run record should describe the changes from the previous model and the decisions taken and could include a brief, but interpretable, description of the run, the objective function value and information whether the model converged successfully. Preferably, the run record should also include parameter estimates (for key runs) and, when needed, a comment about the run.

rules.rule_text: All parameter estimates in the base model, together with their standard errors and/or confidence intervals, must be presented in a table. The inter-individual, residual, and when applicable inter-occasion variability models must also be presented and supported by appropriate graphics.
rules.verbatim_quote: All parameter estimates in the base model, together with their standard errors and/or confidence intervals, should be presented in a table. The inter-individual and residual variability models, and when applicable inter-occasion (intra-individual) variability models, should also be presented and supported by appropriate graphics.

rules.rule_text: GOF plots must be presented for the base model, and when relevant for key stages during base model development. GOF plots appropriate for the analysis must be chosen.
rules.verbatim_quote: GOF plots should be presented for the base model, and when relevant for key stages during base model development [17, 18]. It is recommended to choose GOF plots appropriate for the analysis, as the value of different GOF plots may be dependent on the situation (e.g. rich or sparse data, type of estimation method [19]).
rules.exceptions: When appropriate, simulations can be used for diagnostic purposes to support the value of some GOF plots.

rules.rule_text: GOF plots for the base model must include as a minimum: Observed Data versus Predicted Data (DV vs. PRED) with line of identity and trend line in both linear and log scale; Observed Data versus Individual Predicted Data (DV vs. IPRED) with line of identity and trend line in both linear and log scale; Weighted or Conditional Weighted Residuals versus Predicted Data (WRES or CWRES vs. PRED) with zero line and trend line; Weighted or Conditional Weighted Residuals versus Time (WRES or CWRES vs. TIME) with zero line and trend line; Absolute Individual Weighted Residuals versus Individual Predicted Data (|IWRES| vs. IPRED) with trend line; histogram and/or QQ plot of Weighted Residuals; Observed, Individual Predicted, and Population Predicted concentrations versus Time.
rules.verbatim_quote: Observed Data versus Predicted Data (DV vs. PRED). A line of identity and a trend line should be included. The plot should preferably be provided in both linear and log scale. Observed Data versus Individual Predicted Data (DV vs. IPRED). A line of identity and a trend line should be included. The plot should preferably be provided in both linear and log scale. Weighted Residuals or Conditional Weighted Residuals versus Predicted Data (WRES or CWRES vs. PRED) A zero line and a trend line should be included. Weighted Residuals or Conditional Weighted Residuals versus Time (WRES or CWRES vs. TIME). A zero line and a trend line should be included. Time can be both Time After Dose (TAD) and continuous Time (time in the study). Absolute Individual Weighted Residuals versus Individual Predicted Data (|IWRES| vs. IPRED). A trend line should be included. A histogram and/or QQ plot of the Weighted Residuals. Observed (DV), Individual Predicted (IPRED) and Population Predicted (PRED) concentrations versus Time (overlayed and/or side by side).
rules.exceptions: Plots are preferably in both linear and log scale; exact selection depends on analysis type (rich/sparse data, estimation method).

rules.rule_text: The selection of GOF plots included in the report must be justified and the report must include interpretation of the provided GOF plots.
rules.verbatim_quote: The selection of GOF plots included in the report should be justified [20]. The report should include interpretation of the provided GOF plots.
rules.exceptions: Other plots to support selection of various aspects of the base model may be included or substituted as needed.

rules.rule_text: Any lines of identity, zero lines, or trend lines must be clearly visible in all plots, which could be achieved by using colour.
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be ensured that any lines of identity, zero lines or trend lines are clearly visible in all plots, which could be achieved by e.g. using colour in the plots.

rules.rule_text: If a structural model is selected that does not have the lowest objective function value, the reasons for accepting the simpler model must be clearly presented and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a structural model is selected that does not have the lowest objective function value, the reasons for accepting the simpler model with the higher objective function value should be clearly presented and justified (for example including graphics describing the difference or lack of difference in GOF).

rules.rule_text: Plots generated to screen for potential covariate relationships must be provided in the report.
rules.verbatim_quote: Plots generated to screen for potential covariate relationships should be provided in the report (for example, plots of the empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters versus potential covariates, or the empirical Bayes estimates of the etas versus potential covariates).
rules.exceptions: The value of using empirical Bayes estimates should be assessed, e.g. an assessment of the shrinkage of the individual estimates towards the population mean.

rules.rule_text: The covariate model building steps (forward inclusion and/or backwards deletion) must be clearly presented in a separate run record, including covariates included in the final model and those tested but not retained, with the criteria for decisions (e.g. objective function values) outlined.
rules.verbatim_quote: The covariate model building steps (e.g. both forward inclusion and/or backwards deletion) to illustrate covariates that are included in the final model and those that were tested but were not retained in the final model should be clearly presented in a separate run record. The criteria on which the decision was based, e.g. objective function values, should be outlined as well.

rules.rule_text: The results for the final covariate model must be presented in terms of parameter estimates and in graphical form, showing values of the affected parameter at the extremes and/or the 5-95 percentiles of the covariate range.
rules.verbatim_quote: The results for the final covariate model should be presented in terms of parameter estimates and in graphical form. The values of the affected parameter at the extremes and/or the 5-95 percentiles of the covariate range could also be presented.
rules.exceptions: If many covariates have been included it may be useful to perform simulations to illustrate the effect of various covariate combinations.

rules.rule_text: If a covariate is claimed in labelling to have no effect, adequate and convincing support must be presented, including a confidence interval for the estimated effect, preferably obtained by methods not assuming symmetrical distribution (e.g. bootstrapping or log-likelihood profiling). A conclusion of no effect based solely on graphical screening plots using empirical Bayes estimates is not acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a covariate, in the labelling, is claimed to have no effect, adequate and convincing support of such claim should be presented. For example, a confidence interval for the estimated effect should be provided, preferably obtained using methods that do not assume symmetrical distribution of the confidence interval, e.g. bootstrapping or log-likelihood profiling [9, 10, 11, 12]. A conclusion of no effect based solely upon inspection of graphical screening plots using empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters is usually not acceptable, as shrinkage of the individual estimates towards the population mean may hide true relationships [21].

rules.rule_text: The final model must be clearly described and parameter estimates for all parameters, together with their standard errors and/or confidence intervals, must be presented. The extent to which inter-individual variability and inter-occasion variability are decreased by inclusion of covariates must be stated.
rules.verbatim_quote: The final model should be clearly described and the parameter estimates for all parameters in the final model, together with their standard errors and/or confidence intervals should be presented. It should be stated to what extent inter-individual variability and inter-occasion variability are decreased by inclusion of covariates in the model.

rules.rule_text: The fundamental GOF plots as defined for the base model must also be provided for the final model, including GOF plots for relevant sub-populations.
rules.verbatim_quote: The fundamental GOF plots as defined for the base model should also be supplied. Consider also to present GOF plots for relevant sub-populations.
rules.exceptions: If the final model is identical to the base model, the GOF plots provided for the base model can be referred to in the report.

rules.rule_text: Additional GOF plots for the final model should include: distributions (e.g. histograms) of the empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters and/or eta; a scatter plot matrix of the empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters and/or etas; plots of empirical Bayes estimates of etas versus the covariates in the final model; individual plots that illustrate how well the model describes the data for any given subject.
rules.verbatim_quote: Additional GOF plots for the final model could include: the distributions (e.g. histograms) of the empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters and/or eta, a scatter plot matrix of the empirical Bayes estimates of the parameters and/or etas; plots of empirical Bayes estimates of etas versus the covariates in the final model; individual plots that illustrate how well the model describes the data for any given subject.

rules.rule_text: Evaluation of the effect of omitted data (outliers) must be presented according to the procedures planned for these data.
rules.verbatim_quote: Evaluation of the effect of omitted data (outliers) should be presented according to the procedures planned to be taken for these data.

rules.rule_text: The NONMEM input and output files for the base and final models must be provided, preferably in an appendix, and also electronically as text files.
rules.verbatim_quote: The NONMEM input and output files for the base and final models should be provided, preferably in an appendix. These files should also be provided electronically as text files.
rules.exceptions: Applies when NONMEM is used; if other programs are used the reader can generalise the points to the software used.

rules.rule_text: The outcome of model evaluation procedures must be presented in a manner appropriate to demonstrate that the final model is robust and a sufficiently good description of the data so that the objective(s) of the analysis can be met.
rules.verbatim_quote: The outcome of the model evaluation procedures taken should be presented in a manner appropriate to demonstrate that the final model is robust and a sufficiently good description of the data so that the objective(s) of the analysis can be met.

rules.rule_text: Any simulations performed must be presented in detail making it possible to assess the conclusion made based on the simulation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any simulations performed should be presented in detail making it possible to assess conclusion made based on it, for example the simulation to support a dosing recommendation in a specific sub-populations.

rules.rule_text: The discussion must address how well the final model describes the data and the clinical relevance of any covariate influences, consider agreement with previously obtained information, describe how results will be used (e.g. labelling, individualise dosage, define additional studies), and if a large number of drop-outs occurred, discuss whether this may have affected the results.
rules.verbatim_quote: The discussion of a population PK report should address how well the final model describes the data and the clinical relevance of any covariate influences. The discussion could also consider how well the results of the population PK analysis agree with previously obtained information. A discussion of how the results of the analysis will be used (e.g., to support labelling, individualize dosage, or define additional studies) could be provided. In case of a large number of drop-outs during the study, the report should include a discussion whether this may have affected the results of the analysis.
rules.exceptions: Discussion of drop-out impact only required in case of a large number of drop-outs.

rules.rule_text: GOF plots for the base and final models may be given in parallel to present the improvement by covariate inclusion, and titles should indicate that plots are for both base and final models when the final model is identical to the base model.
rules.verbatim_quote: GOF plots for the base and final model could be given in parallel to present the improvement by covariate inclusion. In case the final model does not include covariates (and is identical to the base model), the GOF plots provided for the base model can be referred to in the report. Titles should indicate that the plots are for both base and final models.
